# Flagr

> Open-source Go service for feature flags, A/B testing, and dynamic configuration.
> One evaluation API (`POST` / `GET /api/v1/evaluation`) returns a sticky variant
> and optional JSON attachment for any entity + context.

Flagr is best for platform and backend teams that want a self-hosted, vendor-neutral
control plane rather than a managed experimentation SaaS. One flag primitive covers
kill switches, progressive rollouts, A/B tests, and runtime config.

## Try it

- Docker: `docker run -it -p 18000:18000 ghcr.io/openflagr/flagr`
- Hosted demo (cold start possible): https://try-flagr.onrender.com
- Source: https://github.com/openflagr/flagr
- License: Apache-2.0 (free to self-host; infra cost is yours)

## Docs

- Home: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/
- Integration guide: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/integration
- Behavioral contracts: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_behavioral_contracts
- Overview: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_overview
- Use cases: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_use_cases
- Built-in context injection: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_injected_context
- Self-hosting: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_self_host
- Environment variables: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_env
- JSON flag source (GitOps): https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_json_flag_spec
- Exposure logging: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_exposure
- Data recorders: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_eval_exposure_pipeline
- Datar analytics: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/flagr_datar
- API reference (OpenAPI / ReDoc): https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/api_docs/
- Contributing: https://openflagr.github.io/flagr/CONTRIBUTING

## Key concepts

- Evaluation assigns a variant; exposure records that the experience was actually shown.
- Sticky assignment uses entityID + flag configuration (no per-user state store required).
- Optional variantAttachment JSON enables dynamic configuration on the same eval call.
- Eval-only / GitOps mode can load flags from JSON (file or HTTP) with CI validation via flagr-validate.
- Built-in context keys (`@ts*`, `@http_*`) inject time and HTTP-derived attributes server-side.

## Optional

- Full repository: https://github.com/openflagr/flagr
- DeepWiki: https://deepwiki.com/openflagr/flagr
